generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance CranePositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor measuring real-time position (X, Y, Z axes) of the gantry crane pucks/containers with high-frequency sampling for kinematic validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CranePositionDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneVelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor capturing real-time velocity measurements of crane actuators and pucks for dynamic control validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneLoadSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force/torque sensor measuring load status of pucks/containers to enable adaptive control and safety validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneLoadDataTransmitted
]

instance CraneAccelerationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Acceleration sensor providing jerk and acceleration data for energy-efficient trajectory optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance CranePositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CranePositionSensor
]

instance CraneVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneVelocitySensor
]

instance CraneLoadDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneLoadSensor
]

instance CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CraneAccelerationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Parametric kinematic model of the gantry crane implemented using FreeCAD and Python-based solvers (NumPy/SciPy) for real-time trajectory computation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CraneSimulationEnabler
]

instance EnergyConsumptionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-physics model combining kinematic and power consumption dynamics for energy-efficient trajectory optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlOptimizationEnabler
]

instance CraneStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured time-series data containing synchronized position, velocity, acceleration, and load measurements with timestamped metadata for post-analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CranePositionDataTransmitted, CraneVelocityDataTransmitted, CraneLoadDataTransmitted, CraneAccelerationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataLoggingEnabler, VisualizationEnabler, ValidationEnabler
]

instance HistoricalTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Longitudinal dataset of executed crane trajectories stored in TimescaleDB for performance benchmarking and anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance SimulationTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Predicted trajectories generated by the digital twin simulation service for comparison with physical measurements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance CraneSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS-based simulation environment using Gazebo for hardware-in-the-loop testing and co-simulation with the physical crane"
    DTDFVocab:enables CraneSimulationService
]

instance ControlOptimizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python-based optimization framework using SciPy and Control Systems Toolbox for real-time MPC and PID tuning"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance DataLoggingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "TimescaleDB-backed logging application with MQTT integration for high-frequency sensor data storage and retrieval"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService
]

instance VisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Three.js-based web visualization framework synchronized with physical movements using WebSocket for real-time rendering"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance ValidationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python validation framework using Euclidean distance metrics to compare predicted vs. physical trajectories with adaptive thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance DarTwinEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured documentation framework for tracking system evolution (hardware/software updates) and their impact on the digital twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemEvolutionService
]

// C6: Services
instance CraneSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "ROS-based simulation environment using Gazebo for hardware-in-the-loop testing and co-simulation with the physical crane"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationInsight
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time control optimization service dynamically adjusting crane motion parameters (acceleration, jerk) to minimize energy consumption or maximize precision via bidirectional communication with the physical controller"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalControlInsight, AdaptiveControlAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Time-series data storage and retrieval service logging operational data with timestamped metadata for post-analysis and longitudinal validation"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceBenchmarkingInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D visualization service rendering synchronized CAD models with real-time sensor overlays for intuitive debugging and training"
    DTDFVocab:provides CraneStateVisualizationInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time model validation service comparing digital twin predictions with physical measurements to quantify discrepancies and detect system drifts"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationResultsInsight, FaultDetectionAction
]

instance SystemEvolutionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structured documentation framework service tracking system evolution and enabling versioning of twin models for reproducibility"
    DTDFVocab:provides EvolutionTrackingInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance SimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation results and co-simulation validation metrics for hardware-in-the-loop testing"
]

instance OptimalControlInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized motion parameters (acceleration profiles, jerk limits) derived from energy-efficient trajectory planning"
]

instance AdaptiveControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of crane control parameters based on real-time validation results"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceBenchmarkingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Comparative analysis metrics between logged operational data and simulated predictions for performance validation"
]

instance CraneStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of crane kinematics with sensor data overlays for intuitive system monitoring"
]

instance ValidationResultsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative metrics comparing digital twin predictions with physical measurements including error thresholds and drift detection"
]

instance FaultDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic alert generation when validation discrepancies exceed predefined thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EvolutionTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured documentation of system updates and their impact on digital twin fidelity using DarTwin notation"
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance CraneControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with crane actuators to execute optimized trajectories received from the digital twin via bidirectional communication"
]

// C1: Environment
instance GantryCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Lab-scale cyber-physical system environment containing the physical gantry crane and its digital twin components"
    base:contains CranePositionSensor, CraneVelocitySensor, CraneLoadSensor, CraneAccelerationSensor, CraneControllerActuator, GantryCranePhysicalComponent
]

instance GantryCranePhysicalComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Mechanical gantry crane structure with kinematic capabilities for movement along X, Y, and Z axes serving as the physical counterpart to the digital twin"
    base:isContainedIn GantryCraneEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** is facilitated by the **Control Application**, which generates and transmits **control commands** to the **Physical Crane Interface** to execute trajectories. Command types include **optimal movement trajectories** (generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service**) designed to eliminate residual swinging motion at the end of movements. The **control message format** is not explicitly detailed, but the system implies structured JSON or protocol buffers (common in Python-based systems) for inter-service communication via the **MQTT Broker**.

**Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly described, but the **Continuous Validation Service** ensures command execution fidelity by comparing simulated (virtual) and executed (physical) trajectories. **Feedback systems** are embedded in the **Logging Application**, which continuously monitors crane state, and the **Dashboard** provides real-time visualization for operator intervention. **Conflict resolution** is not detailed, but the **Continuous Validation Service** acts as a safeguard by alerting operators via the **Dashboard** if trajectory deviations exceed thresholds. **Command execution confirmation** is implied through the validation service’s comparison logic, though explicit acknowledgment protocols (e.g., MQTT QoS levels or handshake mechanisms) are not mentioned.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin systems in the documents employ **ad-hoc orchestration patterns** rather than standardized digital twin frameworks. **GreenhouseDT** uses **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, **ActiveMQ** for message brokering, and **virtual machines (VMs)** with pre-recorded data to facilitate synchronization between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The system relies on **containerization or virtualization** for deployment, with **no explicit mention of orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, Docker Swarm). **Air Quality Management** leverages **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling, integrates with **Raspberry Pi sensors**, and employs **Microsoft Azure tools** for pipeline implementation, including runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction. The system lacks detailed descriptions of **component integration patterns** or **resource management strategies**, but the use of **Azure** suggests cloud-based orchestration. The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** system is described using the **C4 Model**, implying a **containerized microservices architecture** with potential for **decentralized coordination** via container orchestration (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes), though no specific tools or patterns are mentioned. Overall, the systems exhibit **loose coupling** between physical and digital components, with **no formalized constellation patterns** beyond ad-hoc toolchains."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly detail lifecycle stages (e.g., design, deployment, operation, retirement) for any of the Digital Twin systems. However, the **GreenhouseDT** exemplar implies a lifecycle involving:
  - **Development**: Behavioral adaptation of both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, with prerecorded data in virtual machine images for easy deployment.
  - **Deployment**: Use of **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage and **ActiveMQ** for message brokerage, enabling real-time synchronization between the physical and digital systems.
  - **Operation**: Self-adaptive mechanisms for both the greenhouse and its digital twin, though lifecycle governance (e.g., versioning, compliance, or retirement protocols) is not specified.
  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, suggesting lifecycle phases centered around **runtime adaptation** (e.g., sensor data ingestion via Raspberry Pi) and **simulation-driven decision support** (e.g., virus spread prediction). No explicit governance or transition management is documented.
  The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** system is described using the **C4 Model** for architecture visualization but lacks lifecycle stage details beyond architectural decomposition. **Not in Document** for standardized lifecycle governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010 or IEEE 1471)."
]

    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar emphasizes **self-adaptive behavioral alignment** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, implying dynamic validation mechanisms. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on **model-driven engineering** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, suggesting fidelity is ensured through **AutomationML-based model consistency**, but no specific validation protocols (e.g., statistical testing, uncertainty bounds) are mentioned. The **Gantry Crane** exemplar does not address fidelity or validity considerations in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Technical Connection** relies on a **hybrid network architecture** integrating the **Physical Crane** (hardware layer) with digital services via the **MQTT Broker** (message broker) and **TimescaleDB** (time-series database). The **communication protocols** include:
1. **MQTT** for inter-service signaling (e.g., trajectory commands, sensor data, validation alerts) between containers like the **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service**.
2. **Custom Python interfaces** for direct hardware control (e.g., **Physical Crane Interface** communicating with crane controllers).

**Network topology** is not explicitly detailed, but the system implies a **centralized broker-based topology** (MQTT) with potential **point-to-point connections** for critical services (e.g., Control Application ↔ Physical Crane Interface). **Bandwidth and latency requirements** are inferred to be moderate, given the real-time nature of trajectory generation and validation, though exact specifications (e.g., <100ms latency) are absent. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not described, but the **Continuous Validation Service** ensures operational resilience by detecting and alerting on deviations. **Security protocols** are not mentioned, though MQTT typically supports TLS for encrypted communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is **on-premise** (implied by the lack of cloud/edge references) and consists of **five physical containers** (hardware) and **six digital containers** (software services). **Computational requirements** are not quantified, but the system includes:
- A **TimescaleDB** instance for time-series data storage (scalable for high-frequency logging).
- **Grafana Dashboard** for real-time visualization (lightweight but requires moderate compute).
- **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** (likely running on a single or shared compute node with Python dependencies).

**Scalability mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed, but the modular C4 architecture suggests potential horizontal scaling for services (e.g., multiple instances of the **Trajectory Generator Service**). **Deployment models** include:
1. **Monolithic deployment** (all services co-located on a single machine, given the lack of containerization references beyond the C4 model abstraction).
2. **Service-oriented deployment** (each container as a separate process or lightweight container, though not Docker-based).

**Availability/reliability assurance** is ensured via the **Continuous Validation Service**, which monitors system health and triggers alerts. No redundancy or failover protocols (e.g., load balancing, backup databases) are mentioned.
"
]

    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar integrates with **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker) for data exchange, relying on **RESTful APIs** or **MQTT-like protocols** (implied by message brokering) for sensor-actuator communication.   - **GreenhouseDT**:

  **Not in Document** for industry-specific standards (e.g., **IEC 62424** for process automation) or compliance frameworks (e.g., **NIST Digital Twin Reference Architecture**)."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, which inherently includes basic security measures such as role-based access control (RBAC) and data encryption at rest (via TLS/SSL for data in transit). However, explicit documentation on **authentication systems** (e.g., OAuth2, JWT) or **threat mitigation strategies** (e.g., DDoS protection, intrusion detection) is not provided. The system relies on **ActiveMQ** for message brokering, which supports **TLS for secure communication** between components but lacks detailed disclosure of **fail-safe mechanisms** or **risk assessment frameworks**.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar utilizes **Microsoft Azure tools**, which include built-in security features such as **Azure Active Directory (AAD) for authentication**, **role-based access control (RBAC)**, and **data encryption** (both at rest and in transit). The exemplar integrates **Raspberry Pi sensors** for real-time data collection, but no explicit mention of **privacy protection measures** (e.g., GDPR compliance, data anonymization) or **data sharing agreements** is provided. The **AutomationML-based digital twin** may imply standardized data exchange protocols, but **data ownership policies** or **consent management** are not documented.

The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** exemplar does not explicitly describe security or safety considerations in the provided context. No details on **cybersecurity protocols**, **access control mechanisms**, or **safety-critical system designs** (e.g., emergency stop protocols, redundancy checks) are mentioned in the documents.
"
]
